Text[0]: Merry Starlight, friend! The Starlight Stalls are back this year, I'm pleased to say. Make sure ye don't miss 'em!
I hope you enjoy the Starlight Stalls to the fullest, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>!
Text[1]: I shall head there myself shortly to try the new varieties of punch. At this time of year, few things are better than warming oneself up with a hot, delicious drink!

Text[0]: It's so exciting thinking about all the new places out there! I'll never forget this feeling no matter how many places I see!
The Starlight Stalls are in full swing! Will you be heading there?
Merchants from all over Gridania are represented here at the Starlight Stalls!
Hello, my dear. The prizes for this All Saints' Wake's most raucous revelers aren't ready just yet, but you can soak in the haunting atmosphere while you wait!
<sigh> Where's a capable hunter when you need one?
I'm counting on you to fell that beast!
Back, are you? Couldn't stay away from the bright lights, eh?
You must try...<crunch>...these tacos! <munch> Absolutely worth...<crunch>...incarceration! <munch>
Zzzzzz... Mmmmmm...tacos...
Don't worry, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>, I'm working on the storage device. I'll have more data cracked open soon enough.
Text[1]: By the by, I've been feeling eyes on me lately... Not sure who it is, but the softy would probably think it's an admirer.

Text[0]: Ah, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>. I apologize for letting my fear overwhelm me.
Text[1]: The snow spirits do still appear on occasion, but I always flee to Warmth before they get too close.
Text[2]: I hope the day comes again soon that we can welcome their presence, and I promise not to abandon my work here until it does.

Text[0]: Of course, the glyphs we carve into our stones have individual meanings, but their arrangement also affects how they are interpreted in complex ways.
Text[1]: It takes time to decipher the stories they tell, but their nature also means we can express sweeping tales in a small space. There is much work still to be done, so we won't be idle while Fahrafahr studies the sepulchre.

Text[0]: Have you seen the cornfields near the cliffs? I hear the variety growing there is perfectly suited for cold weather.
Text[1]: With most of the construction work already finished, some of our workers have chosen to help with raising the crops. I have faith that soon the spirits and Yok Huy will live in fellowship again.

Text[0]: Thanks to the efforts of all, the snow spirits are finally back to their playful selves. There is no reason for me to flee from them any longer.
Playing with the spirits on my breaks has certainly made my days more lively. The time they almost frightened me off the mountain seems like a distant dream, now...
Text[1]: Even so, I know that they are to be respected. They have dwelled in Urqopacha since ancient times, after all, and within them lies a ferocity which is the very embodiment of nature's wrath.
Text[2]: I intend to stay here and continue my stonework, so that we may never again bear witness to that ferocity.

Text[0]: Greetings, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>. I am always glad when you stop by to view our progress.
Text[1]: The larder has proven invaluable for us. Having a place to store our food means fewer trips hauling heavy loads up the mountain.
Text[2]: If only we knew what that unsavory corn is for... Well, I'm certain Fahrafahr will figure that out in time.

Text[0]: I had a feeling no living thing was meant to eat that corn as it is. But if its purpose is to make kirkahryar, then I understand why it was grown.
Text[1]: I should have guessed... The drink is one of our traditional libations, and has been favored by generations of Yok Huy. I hear they call it something else in Tuliyollal─“chicha,” I think it was.
Text[2]: Seems Dartorgor and the other keepers went to great lengths to keep the spirits happy. It speaks to the strength of the bond they shared with their icy neighbors.

Text[0]: Putting the sepulchre at the altar seems to have had a calming effect on the spirits. I see them playing in the snow at all hours of the day.
Text[1]: I imagine Dartorgor must be proud to see the sight of her dear friends so carefree.

Text[0]: <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>, you wouldn't believe what happened the other day. I was playing with the snow spirits, and they called me by my name!
Text[1]: I've been grinning ever since, remembering the moment. Hah, I suppose I've grown fond of the little things!

Text[0]: We remember playing with the crafty one in Worqor Zormor! The crafty one plays rough, but knows how to have fun!
We will call you the crafty one, yes? Because you can make anything with just your hands!
Text[1]: Never stop building. Build and build, until everyone has such pretty places to play at!

Text[0]: Especially in such harsh environs as these, one requires entertainment that can be enjoyed anywhere. We relax after a hard day's work by indulging in games from distant lands.
A warm welcome to you, adventurer. We of the Continental Circus are pleased to present you with a rousing All Saints' masquerade you'll not soon forget!
My village's leaders ordered me to travel here and learn more of the celebrations you hold in Eorzea─rather intriguing things, all told.
Text[1]: When I return home, we'll have our own rites and such to keep busy with... But until then, I plan to make the most of this “All Saints' Wake” of yours.

Text[0]: Good to see you, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>! This city truly lives in harmony with nature, no? It reminds me so much of my own village that I can't help but feel at home here.
Text[1]: I've not witnessed a celebration like this before, what with all these monstrous decorations scattered about. I assume they're not meant for target practice, correct...?

Text[0]: Oh, I'm so pleased to see you, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>! I hope you've been enjoying the festivities. I've never seen so many pumpkins in one place before!
Text[1]: In fact, I...may have thoughtlessly brandished my spear at a giant pumpkin-headed doll a while ago. He wasn't even causing trouble, so I felt awful about it! Ellerete and Kokonowa's mammets still have a hold on me, it seems...

Text[0]: <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>! Why did you not mention this festival while we were traveling together!? ...Yes, we did have far more pressing concerns at the time, but still! The pumpkin decorations are all so utterly charming!
Text[1]: In any event, I'm glad I managed to find you here. Kaqool Ja has been released, as you can see!
Text[2]: Once the festivities have concluded, he and I plan to travel home together. He did promise to visit my village first, after all!

Text[0]: With the Unbound taken care of, I thought it ideal to roam around Eorzea with the others! We hadn't realized there would be a celebration taking place, so you can imagine our surprise when we arrived.
Text[1]: I've heard they're hosting a masquerade inside a haunted manor, so I'd like to experience it for myself. I'm certain my parents would be thrilled to hear of my adventures there as well.

Text[0]: The Continental Circus revels on in the Haunted Manor! Shall I guide you to their masquerade, dear guest?

“It is important to set aside your duties as you travel, that you might observe the sights and sounds of distant lands more keenly.” So our leaders told us, though I suspect Tentoawa has paid it little mind.
Text[1]: My own worries aside, we've never had the opportunity to travel together, so this has been a lovely experience for us both. I wonder if I should try the rumored transformation magick here for myself...

Text[0]: It has been some time, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>. When I wrote to Ceetol Ja of my release, she was eager to cross the salt once more and find me. In the end, we decided to meet here in Gridania.
Text[1]: Though I've spent some time in Eorzea, I've had few chances to participate in your festivals. Ceetol Ja, at least, seems to be enjoying it all...so I can't ask for much more than that.

Text[0]: I have been instructed to help howsoever I am able. So if you ever need materials, you only need ask!
Should you come into possession of additional Arkveld scales, be sure to bring them to the smithy. He can offer you various goods in exchange.

Little by little, Zirgorteh the Open-armed is beginning to look as it must have when Dartorgor and her kin lived there. For the sakes of Yok Huy and snow spirits alike, I hope we can count on your aid again.
I am quite impressed by how diligently our stonemasons work. Each one of them seems to carry on Dartorgor's legacy in their own way.
Dartorgor's living sepulchre has revealed to me a part of her that even I never knew.
We are but one step away from seeing the spirits appeased. In this most crucial of times, let us tread with care.
In no small part thanks to your efforts, our bond with the spirits has been rekindled. I shall continue the Pact and keep the altar well maintained, so we might never again suffer the spirits' wrath.
Apparently the snow spirits have even been sighted in Worlar's Echo. They must be curious to see what Yok Huy are like outside of Zirgorteh the Open-armed.
Text[1]: From now on, we will have many chances to create new memories together. I look forward to seeing them all engraved upon the living sepulchre.

Text[0]: Each of our volunteers is here of their own accord, and I could not ask for more from them. I hope I can continue to rely on your aid, as well.
With Masons' Work restored, there are tasks big and small to take care of around the workshop. It's demanding, to be sure, but I find it incredibly worthwhile.
The larder has been a great improvement to our lives here. We Yok Huy have hearty appetites to sustain our large bodies, so I suspect there will be plenty of upkeep going forward.
It seems thanks to your help, we can look forward to performing the Pact soon enough. I shall do what I can to ensure that everything else around here runs smoothly.
The snow spirits are appeased for now, but the altar must be maintained if we are to continue the Pact.  After all the work we did, it would be a shame to see the spirits lose themselves again.
While we are more than capable of looking after things ourselves now, any help you could give would be appreciated. Everyone here loves seeing an artisan of your caliber at work.
You should have a few wards to spend by now. You're welcome to browse my wares, if you are so inclined.
Nopli's taken a special interest in the restoration. And if he's interested, then I'll do whatever I can not to disappoint him!
We've received a variety of materials from Worlar's Echo and beyond. Wurfurtar and I are dividing them up and getting them into the right hands.
I admit, fixing gear for an artisan such as you has me a little nervous, but I promise to have it back to you good as new!
You're the crafter helping out Fahrafahr, yes? Thank you for helping her solve this troubling problem.
Why don't we take our minds off of labor with some Triple Triad? I only just learned the rules from one of the merchants, but I'll do my best to give you a good match.
Oh, I won! But was it beginner's luck, or do I have a talent for this? I won't know until I play again, I suppose.
What a thrilling match! The joy of victory...the heartbreak of defeat...both are worthy of being immortalized on my living sepulchre!
Each fierce assault was answered with an equally ferocious counterattack... I'm beginning to see why people like this game! I would be honored to have another bout with you.
A project of this size will entail tasks of all sizes. I must first assess the skills of our volunteers to see just how much we can accomplish on our own.
